protein.notes: Normal protein intake; high-quality protein preferred

potassium.notes: No restriction unless hyperkalemia present; monitor levels

phosphorus.notes: Limit processed foods high in phosphate additives

calcium.notes: Normal intake from diet; supplement if deficient

protein.notes: Reduce protein to slow progression; 50% high biological value

potassium.notes: Moderate restriction; monitor serum K+; leach vegetables

phosphorus.notes: Avoid phosphate additives (cola drinks, processed cheese, instant meals)

sodium.notes: Strict sodium control for BP management

fluid.notes: Usually unrestricted; monitor if edema present

calcium.notes: Monitor levels; vitamin D supplementation often needed

protein.notes: Very low protein diet (VLPD); consider keto-acid supplements

potassium.notes: Strict restriction; avoid salt substitutes (KCl); leach/boil vegetables

phosphorus.notes: Phosphate binders likely needed; avoid all phosphate additives

sodium.notes: Very strict; for hypertension and fluid balance

fluid.notes: Restrict if urine output declining; weigh daily

calories.notes: Higher calorie target to prevent PEW (protein-energy wasting)

protein.notes: If on dialysis: INCREASE protein to 1.2g/kg (dialysis removes amino acids)

potassium.notes: Very strict; potentially life-threatening hyperkalemia risk

phosphorus.notes: Multiple phosphate binders required; dialysis removes some phosphorus

sodium.notes: Severe restriction for fluid management between dialysis sessions

fluid.notes: Restrict to urine output + 500mL; weigh twice daily

calories.notes: Critical to prevent malnutrition; dialysis patients at high PEW risk

calcium.notes: Avoid calcium-based phosphate binders if hypercalcemic

leaching_technique.description: Leaching reduces potassium content of vegetables by 30-50%
steps: Peel and cut vegetables into small pieces
Soak in large volume of warm water for 2-4 hours (or overnight)
Cook in fresh water (don't use soaking water)
